messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Using LLMs for R coding and package development - livestream seminar
messages.subject_clean: Using LLMs for R coding and package development - livestream seminar
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Instats is thrilled to offer a 3-day seminar, Using LLMs for R Coding and Package Development <https://instats.org/seminar/using-llms-for-r-coding-and-package-deve>, running May 20 - 22 by professors Seth Flaxman from the University of Oxford and Leonid Chindelevitch from Imperial College London. This seminar is designed to equip researchers and PhD students with the skills necessary to efficiently integrate Large Language Models (LLMs) into their research workflows using R. By mastering tools such as ChatGPT and Claude, participants will gain a competitive edge in R coding and package development. The seminar allows participants to explore practical applications, engage in real-world coding scenarios, and learn techniques for optimizing R code. Harness the synergy of traditional coding and AI-powered tools to transform your research methodologies, while enhancing your coding efficiency and staying at the forefront of technological advances in data science and quantitative research.

Sign up today <https://instats.org/seminar/using-llms-for-r-coding-and-package-deve> to secure your spot, and please share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
instats.org <http://instats.org>

Follow Instats:?Bluesky <https://bsky.app/profile/instats.bsky.social>_Linkedin <https://linkedin.com/company/instats1>_Facebook <https://www.facebook.com/InstatsTraining/>

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Introduction to Genome-Wide Association Studies (GWAS) course
messages.subject_clean: Introduction to Genome-Wide Association Studies (GWAS) course
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,

 
We are happy to announce the upcoming Physalia online course on "Introduction to Genome-Wide Association Studies (GWAS)"! If you're looking to enhance your skills and knowledge in GWAS analysis, this course is tailored for you. Whether you are a student, researcher, or professional, this course will provide you with the essential tools to confidently navigate the world of GWAS.
 
Course Dates: Online, 23-27 June 


Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course49/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course49/ )
 
This course will guide you through the entire process of GWAS data analysis, ensuring that you not only understand the individual steps but also learn how to create a reproducible and efficient bioinformatics workflow.
 
Over the span of five days, our course will cover a range of topics through a well-structured module-based approach. Each day will commence with informative lectures, followed by interactive class discussions to solidify key concepts. Subsequently, you will engage in hands-on practical sessions designed to foster your skills. These sessions will include both guided exercises with our experienced instructors and independent tasks to reinforce your learning. After each exercise, you will have the opportunity to interpret and discuss results within a group setting.

A background in biology, particularly genetics, is recommended. While a basic familiarity with R programming and Unix will be helpful, our course will provide ample support for learners at varying levels of experience.
 
 
For the full list of our our courses and workshops, please have a look at: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/ )
 
 
Best regards,
Carlo
 
 
--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

info at physalia-courses.org

mobile: +49 17645230846

[ Bluesky ]( https://bsky.app/profile/physaliacourses.bsky.social ) [ Linkedin ]( https://www.linkedin.com/in/physalia-courses-a64418127/ )

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Data Visualization with ggplot2 - livestream seminar
messages.subject_clean: Data Visualization with ggplot2 - livestream seminar
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Instats is excited to offer a 1-day seminar, Data Visualization with ggplot2 <https://instats.org/seminar/data-visualization-with-ggplot2>, livestreaming on May 19 and led by the Statistical Methodology and Consultancy Service (SMCS) of UC Louvain. Effective visuals are indispensable for exploring data, uncovering patterns, and communicating results, and ggplot2?the gold-standard R package?lets you turn complex datasets into clear, publication-ready graphics. Tailored to PhD candidates, faculty, and applied researchers, this intensive workshop moves from visualization fundamentals through advanced customization, layering, annotation, and best practices, equipping you to transform raw numbers into persuasive narratives and to streamline your exploratory analysis workflow across disciplines. A basic familiarity with R is recommended, and those needing a refresher can join Instats? free Introduction to R seminar to get up to speed beforehand. 

Sign up today <https://instats.org/seminar/data-visualization-with-ggplot2> to secure your spot, and please share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!

Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
https://instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Upcoming Online Course: Reproducibility in Bioinformatics – July 7–9
messages.subject_clean: Upcoming Online Course: Reproducibility in Bioinformatics – July 7–9
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
We are excited to announce our upcoming online course on Reproducibility in Bioinformatics, taking place from July 7?9,  from 2?8 PM (Berlin time).
 
Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/bioinformatics-reproducibility/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/bioinformatics-reproducibility/ )
 
 
This hands-on course will introduce key strategies to make your bioinformatics workflows more reproducible and transparent. You will learn to use containers, virtual environments, version control, and workflow management systems like Snakemake and Nextflow.
 
Topics include:

Best practices in data organization and documentation

Version control with Git

Using and building containers (Docker/Singularity)

Setting up reproducible workflows

Running pipelines across different computing environments
 
Best regards,Carlo
 
 

--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] online course– Introduction to R and Bioconductor
messages.subject_clean: online course– Introduction to R and Bioconductor
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
We?re excited to share that there are only 2 seats left for our online course: "Introduction to R and Bioconductor"
 
Dates: 26th & 28th May, and 5th & 6th June 2025
 
Time: 9:30 AM ? 12:30 PM (CET)
 
Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/intro-to-bioc/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/intro-to-bioc/ )
 
 
This beginner-friendly course offers hands-on training in R programming, tidyverse data processing, and the Bioconductor ecosystem, tailored to biological and genomic data analysis. No prior experience with R or Bioconductor is required.
 
Attendees ?ll learn how to:

Navigate RStudio and manage data analysis projects

Work with R data structures, tidyverse tools, and visualization packages like ggplot2

Use Bioconductor to handle DNA, RNA, and protein data

Apply key data structures like SummarizedExperiment and SingleCellExperiment
This course is ideal for life science researchers and students starting their journey in R-based bioinformatics.
 
Best regards,
Carlo
 
 
--------------------

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Introduction to Experimental Design - livestream seminar
messages.subject_clean: Introduction to Experimental Design - livestream seminar
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Instats is thrilled to offer a 1-day seminar, Introduction to Experimental Design: Concepts and Applications <https://instats.org/seminar/introduction-to-experimental-design-conc>, livestreaming on May 22 and led by Dr Byran Smucker from the Department of Public Health Sciences, Division of Biostatistics, Henry Ford Health. Experimental design underpins credible, reproducible research across psychology, medicine, engineering, and beyond; this intensive workshop equips academics, graduate students, and industry practitioners with the practical philosophy of experimentation?highlighting randomization, replication, and blocking?while walking through power analysis, one-factor studies, and more advanced designs. Through real-world examples and R-based demonstrations (no prior R experience required), participants will learn to distinguish exploratory from confirmatory studies, avoid common pitfalls, and implement simple yet powerful confirmatory experiments that stand up to rigorous scrutiny.

Sign up today <https://instats.org/seminar/introduction-to-experimental-design-conc> to secure your spot, and please share with colleagues and students who may benefit!

Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
https://instats.org

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

Designed for academics and PhD students, this hands-on workshop shows you how to integrate cutting-edge large language models such as ChatGPT and Claude into your R workflow to accelerate coding, debugging, optimization, and package creation. messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Few Seats Left – Analysing Biodiversity Through Time and Space Using R (9–13 June)
messages.subject_clean: Few Seats Left – Analysing Biodiversity Through Time and Space Using R (9–13 June)
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
We?re excited to announce that there are only a few seats left for our upcoming online course: Analysing Biodiversity Through Time and Space Using R
Dates: 9?13 June 
 
Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/biodiversity-in-r/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/biodiversity-in-r/ )
 
This hands-on course will guide you through building reproducible workflows for acquiring, cleaning, and analysing biodiversity data from both modern and fossil records. You?ll explore how diversity patterns vary across space and time, and learn to address common sampling challenges using powerful R tools and packages.
 
By the end of the course, you will be able to:

Access and clean biodiversity occurrence data from major databases (GBIF, PBDB, IUCN)

Analyse biodiversity patterns across spatial and temporal scales

Visualise biodiversity data and estimate diversification rates using R

Integrate palaeontological and neontological datasets for comprehensive biodiversity analysis
 
Best regards, Carlo

--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D

messages.body_plain:
*Unlock the Power of Spatial Data Visualisation in R using TMAP*

Join our focused online course: *?Visual Exploration, Analysis and
Presentation of Spatial Data Using the Tmap Package?
<https://www.prstats.org/course/visual-exploration-analysis-and-presentation-of-spatial-data-using-the-tmap-package-tmap01/>*

*Course dates:* 3-6 June 2025

*Location:* Online Live Course

Whether you?re a researcher, analyst, or data scientist, this course equips
you with the practical skills to:

   -

   Build and customise layered maps using the *Tmap* package in R
   -

   Combine spatial data with analytical results for compelling
   visualisations
   -

   Integrate thematic maps into presentations and publications
   -

   Work confidently with spatial objects and geospatial data sets

*Course Highlights:*

   -

   Hands-on exercises with real data
   -

   Taught by experienced spatial data scientists
   -

   Lifetime access to course materials and recordings
   -

   Suitable for both intermediate R users and those new to spatial analysis

*Next session starts soon ? spaces are limited.*

Secure your place here:

https://www.prstats.org/course/visual-exploration-analysis-and-presentation-of-spatial-data-using-the-tmap-package-tmap01/
-- 
Oliver Hooker PhD.

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] online course on "Introduction to Genome-Wide Association Studies (GWAS)"
messages.subject_clean: online course on "Introduction to Genome-Wide Association Studies (GWAS)"
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
We are pleased to announce our upcoming online course on "Introduction to Genome-Wide Association Studies (GWAS)", taking place from 23 to 27 June 2025.
 
Course page: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course49/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course49/ )
 
Whether you're a student, researcher, or professional looking to build a strong foundation in GWAS analysis, this course is designed to equip you with the core tools and knowledge needed to confidently approach GWAS data and workflows.
 
Over five days, the course will guide you through a comprehensive, module-based program. Each day features a combination of clear, engaging lectures and interactive discussions to reinforce key concepts. These will be followed by hands-on practical sessions, including both guided exercises with our instructors and independent tasks to deepen your understanding. You'll also have opportunities to discuss and interpret your results collaboratively with peers.
 
Participants should ideally have a background in biology, especially genetics. While basic familiarity with R and Unix is recommended, the course is structured to support learners with varying levels of experience.
 
For a complete overview of our training programme, please visit: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/ )
 
 
Best regards,
 Carlo

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Tidyverse for Ecologists (TIDY01)
messages.body_plain:
ONLINE COURSE ? Tidyverse for Ecologists (TIDY01)

https://www.prstats.org/course/tidyverse-for-ecologists-tidy01/

16th - 20th June

Please feel free to share!

COURSE OVERVIEW - This course comprehensively introduces the Tidyverse and
focuses on its use in data science projects. It is designed to give
participants a strong foundation in R programming, core Tidyverse packages,
and the Tidymodels framework. The course emphasises hands-on projects to
apply learned concepts to real-world data analysis and modelling tasks
applied to biology. By the end of the course, participants should:
? Understand the fundamentals of R programming for data analysis.
? Be proficient in using core Tidyverse packages to clean, transform, and
visualise data.
? Gain an introduction to basic machine learning concepts through the
Tidymodels framework.
? Learn to preprocess, build, evaluate, and interpret models using
Tidymodels.
? Apply Tidyverse and Tidymodels tools to solve real-world problems
through hands-on
projects.

Please email oliverhooker at prstatistics.com with any questions

Day 1: A Short Course in R Basics (9:30 - 17:30)
This day provides participants with the foundational R skills required for
working with Tidyverse and
Tidymodels. It is designed for beginners or those needing a refresher in R
programming.
? Section 1 (R Essentials): This section focuses on R syntax, variables,
data types, conditionals (`if`,
`else`, `elif`), loops (`for`, `while`), and writing reusable code using
functions.
? Section 2 (Data Structures and File Handling in R): This section
emphasises understanding data
structures (e.g., vectors, data frames, lists) and handling files by
reading/writing data (e.g., CSVs)
for manipulation and analysis.

Day 2: Fundamentals of Tidyverse I (9:30 - 17:30)
This day introduces participants to the foundational concepts of Tidyverse
packages and their
applications to data science projects.
? Section 3 (Data Manipulation I): This section covers the basics of data
manipulation using `dplyr`
functions such as `filter()`, `select()`, `mutate()`, `arrange()`, and
`summarise ()`. Participants will
learn how to clean, transform, and prepare datasets for analysis.
? Section 4 (Data Visualisation I): This section introduces the principles
of data visualisation using
`ggplot2`. Participants will learn how to create basic plots such as
scatterplots, bar charts, and
line graphs while exploring the grammar of graphics.

Day 3: Fundamentals of Tidyverse II (9:30 - 17:30)
This day builds on the foundations established in Day 2 and dives deeper
into advanced data
manipulation and visualisation techniques.
? Section 5 (Data Manipulation II): This section extends the use of `dplyr`
by introducing more
complex operations such as joins, grouping with `group_by()`, and working
with pipelines using
`%>%`. Finally, additional packages will be presented to enhance data
manipulation
programming.
? Section 6 (Data Visualisation II): Participants will explore advanced
visualisation techniques
using extensions of `ggplot2`, such as creating animated plots with the
`gganimate` package and
interactive visualisations with additional tools.

Day 4: Applying Tidyverse Fundamentals to Data Modelling (9:30 - 17:30)
This day introduces participants to machine learning concepts using core
libraries for statistical modelling
and deep learning.
? Section 7 (Introduction to regression): This section focuses on
regression modelling using
Tidymodels. Participants will learn to implement linear regression models,
evaluate model
performance, and interpret results.
? Section 8 (Introduction to Classification): This section introduces
techniques such as support
vector machines and neural networks using Tidymodels. Participants will
also explore methods
for assessing the performance of classification models.

Day 5: Data Science Workflow with Tidyverse (9:30 - 17:30)
On the final day, participants will apply all their newly acquired skills
to solve real-world problems
inspired by ecological datasets.
? Section 9 (The data science workflow): The workflow will be illustrated
based on the core
packages introduced. The book "R for Data Science" will serve as
a base literature for this day
? Section 10 (Hands-on project): Participants will work through a complete
data science workflow,
including data cleaning, transformation, visualisation, modelling, and
communication of results.

-- 
Oliver Hooker PhD.
PR stats

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Efficient Data Wrangling with R data.table - livestream seminar
messages.subject_clean: Efficient Data Wrangling with R data.table - livestream seminar
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Instats is excited to offer a 1-day seminar, Efficient Data Wrangling with R data.table <https://instats.org/seminar/efficient-data-wrangling-with-r-datatabl>, livestreaming on June 4 and led by Dr Fodil Ihaddaden from the Institute for Statistical and Data Science. Designed for PhD students, academics, and professional researchers who grapple with large datasets, this workshop showcases how the data.table package can radically improve speed and efficiency over base R and dplyr. Participants will explore core syntax, keys and indexing, rapid filtering and mutating, advanced aggregation, memory-conscious handling of big data, grouped operations, and strategies for integrating data.table with other R tools, all through hands-on exercises that translate directly to real research challenges.

Sign up today <https://instats.org/seminar/efficient-data-wrangling-with-r-datatabl> to secure your spot, and feel free to share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!

Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
https://instats.org

messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone,
 
This is the final call to join the Physalia course "A Practical Hands-On Introduction to R with the tidyverse"
Dates: 2?4 June 2025 | 14:00?19:00 Berlin time
 
 
 
Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/r-tidyverse/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/r-tidyverse/ )
 
 
 
Whether you're completely new to coding or looking to strengthen your data wrangling and visualization skills, this course will give you the tools you need to work confidently with R ? all in a friendly, interactive environment.
 
 
 
Over 3 days, you'll learn how to:
 

Import, transform, and visualize data with the tidyverse

Automate tasks and write functions

Create polished reports using Quarto
 
Best regards,
 
Carlo

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Deep Learning with ChatGPT - livestream seminar
messages.subject_clean: Deep Learning with ChatGPT - livestream seminar
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Instats is excited to offer a 1-day seminar, Deep Learning for Complex Datasets with ChatGPT <https://instats.org/seminar/deep-learning-for-complex-datasets-with>, running June 6th and led by professor Nikolay Oskolkov from at Lund University. This intensive workshop equips PhD students, academics, and professional researchers with the practical skills needed to harness cutting-edge deep learning approaches for complex, real-world data. Through hands-on exercises in R, Python, TensorFlow, and Keras?guided by ChatGPT as an interactive coding consultant?you will learn foundational principles of machine and deep learning, build neural networks from scratch, explore convolutional and recurrent architectures for sequential and genomic data, apply autoencoders for dimensionality reduction, and examine case studies spanning multi-omics integration, single-cell biology, and personalized medicine. By the end of the day, participants will be able to design, train, evaluate, and refine their own models, integrate Large Language Models into existing analytical workflows, and take away actionable insights that immediately translate to their research.

Sign up today <https://instats.org/seminar/deep-learning-for-complex-datasets-with> to secure your spot, and feel free to share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
https://instats.org
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]
